question: Name the best-selling fiction authors of all time by estimated copies sold.

answers.display: Agatha Christie — 2 billion+ copies sold (most by any solo fiction author)
J.R.R. Tolkien — 600 million+ copies sold
Stephen King — 350 million+ copies sold
J.K. Rowling — 600 million+ copies sold
Danielle Steel — 800 million+ copies sold

question: Name the best-selling book series of all time by copies sold.

answers.display: Harry Potter (J.K. Rowling) — 600 million+ copies sold
Goosebumps (R.L. Stine) — 400 million+ copies sold
Perry Mason (Erle Stanley Gardner) — 300 million+ copies sold
Berenstain Bears (Stan & Jan Berenstain) — 260 million+ copies sold
Choose Your Own Adventure — 250 million+ copies sold

question: Name the best-selling individual novels of all time.

answers.display: Don Quixote (Cervantes) — 500 million+ copies sold
A Tale of Two Cities (Dickens) — 200 million+ copies sold
The Lord of the Rings (Tolkien) — 150 million+ copies sold
The Little Prince (Saint-Exupéry) — 200 million+ copies sold

answers.text: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
answers.display: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone — 120 million+ copies sold

question: Name the authors who have won the most Pulitzer Prizes for Fiction.

answers.display: Booth Tarkington — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Fiction (The Magnificent Ambersons, Alice Adams)
William Faulkner — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Fiction (A Fable, The Reivers)
John Updike — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Fiction (Rabbit Is Rich, Rabbit at Rest)
Colson Whitehead — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Fiction (The Underground Railroad, The Nickel Boys)
Jhumpa Lahiri — 1 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction (Interpreter of Maladies)

question: Name the countries whose authors have won the most Nobel Prizes in Literature.

answers.display: France — 16 Nobel Prizes in Literature (most of any country)
United States — 13 Nobel Prizes in Literature
United Kingdom — 13 Nobel Prizes in Literature
Germany — 9 Nobel Prizes in Literature
Sweden — 8 Nobel Prizes in Literature

question: Name the authors with the most books on the New York Times Best Sellers list all-time.

answers.display: Nora Roberts — 200+ NYT bestsellers (most of any author)
Stephen King — 60+ NYT bestsellers
John Grisham — 28 consecutive #1 NYT bestsellers (record)
James Patterson — 100+ NYT bestsellers
Danielle Steel — 100+ NYT bestsellers

question: Name the novels that have won both the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction and the National Book Award.

answers.display: Invisible Man (Ralph Ellison, 1953) — National Book Award
To Kill a Mockingbird (Harper Lee, 1961) — Pulitzer Prize
The Color Purple (Alice Walker, 1983) — Pulitzer Prize & National Book Award
Beloved (Toni Morrison, 1988) — Pulitzer Prize
The Underground Railroad (Colson Whitehead, 2016) — Pulitzer Prize & National Book Award

question: Name the best-selling nonfiction books of all time.

answers.display: Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung — 900 million+ copies sold
The Holy Bible — 5 billion+ copies distributed (all editions)
A Brief History of Time (Stephen Hawking) — 25 million+ copies sold
Guinness World Records (annual) — 143 million+ copies sold since 1955

answers.text: The Diary of a Young Girl
answers.display: The Diary of a Young Girl (Anne Frank) — 35 million+ copies sold

question: Name the authors who have won the Man Booker Prize (now the Booker Prize) the most times.

answers.display: Peter Carey — 2 Booker Prize wins (Oscar and Lucinda, True History of the Kelly Gang)
J.M. Coetzee — 2 Booker Prize wins (Life & Times of Michael K, Disgrace)
Hilary Mantel — 2 Booker Prize wins (Wolf Hall, Bring Up the Bodies)
Salman Rushdie — 1 Booker Prize win (Midnight's Children, also won Best of the Booker)
Ian McEwan — 1 Booker Prize win (Amsterdam)

question: Name the best-selling children's book series of all time by copies sold.

answers.display: Harry Potter (J.K. Rowling) — 600 million+ copies sold
Goosebumps (R.L. Stine) — 400 million+ copies sold
Berenstain Bears (Stan & Jan Berenstain) — 260 million+ copies sold
Diary of a Wimpy Kid (Jeff Kinney) — 250 million+ copies sold
The Hunger Games (Suzanne Collins) — 100 million+ copies sold
